extraction.one_line_summary: GAVIRIA Fernando won stage 1 sprinting from 200m out after QuickStep controlled the entire technical finale, SAGAN Peter second unable to close a 3-bike-length gap.
tactical_patterns: QuickStep deployed five riders in final 1.5km on technical finish, owning front throughout stage to neutralize sprint rivals completely.
GAVIRIA took intermediate sprint fourth place, gaining 26-point green jersey lead over SAGAN before stage finish—points jersey strategy prioritized early.
Technical finale geometry favored front position so heavily that SAGAN's three-bike-length deficit at 200m became insurmountable despite pure speed parity.
FROOME Chris crashed inside 5km safe zone but still lost 1:01, suggesting speed/positioning into technical section trumps crash protection rule.

rider_signals.GAVIRIA Fernando: positive: Won with three-length margin from 200m after taking intermediate sprint points, showing both tactical awareness and stage-winning speed reserve.
rider_signals.SAGAN Peter: neutral: Second place despite Bora positioning him closest to GAVIRIA at sprint launch, acknowledging winner's superiority post-finish suggests speed deficit not tactics.
rider_signals.FROOME Chris: negative: Lost 1:01 after crash inside 5km despite time protection rule, revealing poor positioning into technical finale for GC leader.
rider_signals.MATTHEWS Michael: negative: Lost time on stage 1 after strong prior Tour, suggesting positioning vulnerability in chaotic technical finales despite climbing ability.
rider_signals.LEDANOIS Kévin: positive: Won cat 4 KOM sprint from breakaway then immediately sat up to rejoin peloton, showing energy conservation intelligence for jersey defense.
